expectations.text: Returns exactly ten non-duplicate issues spanning at least seven different quality domains.
expectations.evidence: Exactly ten numbered findings cover onboarding, targeting, responsive layout, migration, route graph, content/story variation, reward information, audio, permanent equipment, and deployment/cache.

expectations.text: Each issue contains reproduction/evidence, player impact, root cause, concrete fix, and pass condition.
expectations.evidence: Every numbered finding uses the same explicit fields: 复现、症状、玩家影响、根因、修复、通过条件、旧玩家保护.

expectations.text: Includes at least one save migration issue and one public cache/deployment issue.
expectations.evidence: Finding 4 specifies versioned migration and backup; finding 10 specifies mixed-version cache, deployed commit, build IDs, and stable URL.

expectations.text: Includes at least one strategic-truth issue such as fake routes or weak reward choice, not only UI polish.
expectations.evidence: Finding 5 tests whether route edges truly constrain clicks; finding 7 tests whether rewards support real card/equipment construction.

expectations.text: Explains compatibility protections for active players and stable links.
expectations.evidence: Every issue has an old-player protection; deployment keeps the original URL and migration namespace while adding versioned assets and rollback.

expectations.text: Does not treat HTTP 200 or syntax success as proof of visual/gameplay correctness.
expectations.evidence: Opening rejects link availability as completion; finding 10 explicitly requires build identity and mixed-cache checks beyond HTTP 200.

uncertainties: Hypothesis-driven because source and network inspection were intentionally prohibited.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong and discriminating: it covers strategic truth and release compatibility in addition to transactional bugs.
